description: Conforming exactly to truth or to a standard; free from error. 'Telua' names the quality of correctness—alignment between representation and reality, between claim and fact.
sound_symbolism: The word aligns with truth. 'Te' begins with definite clarity, fact established. Then 'lu' flows toward alignment, matching reality. Finally 'a' opens to confirmation, accuracy achieved.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'telua sano' (accurate knowledge). Related to 'shewo' (true) but emphasizes factual correctness rather than authenticity of being.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Accurate perception sees things as they are, not as we wish them to be—the foundation of insight.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Accurate communication builds trust—misrepresentation, even unintentional, damages relationship.
